Requirement: Which assets compose a water distribution infrastructure? Catchment well, raw water pipe, reservoirs, etc.
A catchment well is a well that has been constructed to collect water.
A wastewater treatment plant is a facility where contaminants are removed from wastewater or sewage.
Which types of sensors are used in water infrastructures? Water quality sensors, capacity sensors, tank level sensors, etc.
Which types of actuators are used in water infrastructures? Pressure regulators, pumps, valves, etc.
A water meter is a device that observes different aspects of a water infrastructure.

There are four main types of water assets: source, sink, transport and storage ones.
Examples of source assets are: lakes, lagoons or glaciers.
Examples of sink assets are: rivers, seas, oceans or estuaries.
An aquifer is a type of storage asset.
An intake is a type of transport asset.
A hydroelectric power plant is a type of water infrastructure.
A gauging station is a type of water monitoring infrastructure.
A water infrastructure is intended for a particular use of water, for example: industry, agriculture, recreation or aquaculture.
Total dissolved solids is an acceptability property of water.
Dissolved oxygen is a chemical property of water.
Some environmental properties are relevant for water, such as external humidity, atmospheric pressure, amount of precipitation.
A device can monitor a water asset.
